summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened a two-part series on thriving relationships, acknowledging Valentine's Day but emphasizing that biblical love should be a daily commitment. He began by reflecting on God's design for companionship from Genesis, noting that everyone desires a thriving, lasting relationship, yet the reality is that no marriage is perfect because no person is perfect. Pastor Travis, or PT, shared candidly from his own 23-year marriage and family history, including the lasting impact of divorce, to illustrate that relationships require work and grace. The core of the message was an exposition of 1 Corinthians 13, focusing on four key attributes of love: patience, kindness, selflessness, and forgiveness. He explained that patience involves both short-term restraint in conflict and long-term endurance for growth, while kindness must be both proactive and reactive, especially in a world that often lacks it. The sermon highlighted that selfishness is a primary relationship killer and that Christ-like love is about giving, not getting—a stark contrast to the world's concept of lust. Finally, PT stressed the critical need for forgiveness, warning that unforgiveness leads to bitterness that can poison one's future. He closed with a prayer for all relationships—whether dating, married, or single—encouraging everyone to pursue Christ first, as He is the foundation for any healthy connection.
mainPoints: Love is patient, requiring both short-term restraint in conflict and long-term endurance for personal and relational growth.
Love is kind, calling for proactive acts of tenderness and a reactive choice to defuse conflict rather than escalate it.
Love is selfless and forgiving, demanding that we lay down our own desires and release past hurts to avoid the poison of bitterness.

versesMentioned.text: Love is patient, love is kind. It does not envy, it does not boast, it is not proud. It does not dishonor others, it is not self-seeking, it is not easily angered, it keeps no record of wrongs. Love does not delight in evil but rejoices with the truth. It always protects, always trusts, always hopes, always perseveres. Love never fails.
Be completely humble and gentle; be patient, bearing with one another in love.
But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, forbearance, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness and self-control. Against such things there is no law.
Be kind and compassionate to one another, forgiving each other, just as in Christ God forgave you.

Submit to one another out of reverence for Christ. Wives, submit yourselves to your own husbands as you do to the Lord. For the husband is the head of the wife as Christ is the head of the church, his body, of which he is the Savior. Now as the church submits to Christ, so also wives should submit to their husbands in everything. Husbands, love your wives, just as Christ loved the church and gave himself up for her.

Therefore, as God’s chosen people, holy and dearly loved, clothe yourselves with compassion, kindness, humility, gentleness and patience. Bear with each other and forgive one another if any of you has a grievance against someone. Forgive as the Lord forgave you.
If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just and will forgive us our sins and purify us from all unrighteousness.

Monday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 13:4 - 'Love is patient, love is kind.'
Monday.focus: Cultivating patience in a specific relationship.
Monday.action: Identify one moment today where you feel impatient. Pause, take a deep breath, and choose a kind response instead.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help me to slow down and extend Your patience to those around me today.

Tuesday.scripture: Ephesians 4:32 - 'Be kind and compassionate to one another, forgiving each other, just as in Christ God forgave you.'
Tuesday.action: Perform one unexpected, kind act for someone you love, without expecting anything in return.
Tuesday.prayer: God, open my eyes to see a need I can meet with a simple act of kindness.

Wednesday.scripture: Ephesians 5:21 - 'Submit to one another out of reverence for Christ.'
Wednesday.action: In a decision today (like what to eat or watch), intentionally defer to the preference of your spouse, friend, or family member.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, teach me to lay down my own desires and serve others as You served us.

Thursday.scripture: Colossians 3:13 - 'Bear with each other and forgive one another if any of you has a grievance against someone. Forgive as the Lord forgave you.'
Thursday.action: Write down one old grievance you're holding onto. Pray to release it to God, then tear up the paper as a symbol of forgiveness.
Thursday.prayer: Father, I choose to forgive as You have forgiven me. Heal my heart and free me from bitterness.

Friday.scripture: Galatians 5:22-23 - 'But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, forbearance, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness and self-control.'
Friday.action: Reflect on this list. Which 'fruit' is strongest in your key relationships? Which needs cultivation? Choose one to focus on this weekend.
Friday.prayer: Holy Spirit, produce Your fruit in me. Make me a source of love, peace, and kindness in my relationships.

Saturday.scripture: 1 John 1:9 - 'If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just and will forgive us our sins and purify us from all unrighteousness.'
Saturday.action: Spend a few moments in quiet confession, receiving God's forgiveness. Then, reach out to someone you may have wronged this week and seek reconciliation.
Saturday.prayer: Thank You, God, for Your complete forgiveness. Give me the humility to seek peace and mend broken connections.
